sentences.sentence: According to the latest figures, the majority of the world's population is now bilingual or multilingual, having grown up speaking two or more languages.
sentences.translation: 根据最新数据，世界上大多数人口现在都是双语或多语使用者，他们从小就说两种或更多语言。

structure.text: According to the latest figures
the majority of the world's population
is now bilingual or multilingual
having grown up speaking two or more languages

difficult_points: According to... 是句首介词短语，引出数据来源而不是句子主干。
the majority of 后接 population，谓语用单数 is，强调“人口中的大多数”。
having grown up... 是现在分词的完成式，补充说明双语或多语状态形成于成长过程。

sentences.sentence: In the past, such children were considered to be at a disadvantage compared with their monolingual peers.
sentences.translation: 过去，人们认为这样的孩子与只会一种语言的同龄人相比处于劣势。

structure.text: In the past
such children
were considered to be at a disadvantage
compared with their monolingual peers

difficult_points: were considered 是被动语态，表示外界对这类儿童的看法。
to be at a disadvantage 作 consider 的补足成分，说明被认为所处的状态。
compared with... 是过去分词短语，引出比较对象。

sentences.sentence: Over the past few decades, however, technological advances have allowed researchers to look more deeply at how bilingualism interacts with and changes the cognitive and neurological systems, thereby identifying several clear benefits of being bilingual.
sentences.translation: 然而，在过去几十年中，技术进步使研究人员能够更深入地观察双语现象如何与认知和神经系统相互作用并改变这些系统，从而识别出双语带来的几项明确好处。

structure.text: Over the past few decades
technological advances
have allowed researchers to look more deeply
at how bilingualism interacts with and changes the cognitive and neurological systems
how bilingualism interacts with and changes the cognitive and neurological systems
thereby identifying several clear benefits of being bilingual

difficult_points: however 插在时间状语后，标志旧观点与新研究发现之间的转折。
allow 后接宾语 researchers 和不定式 to look，表示“使研究人员能够……”。
how 引导的从句作 look at 的宾语，其中 interacts with 和 changes 共用主语 bilingualism。
thereby identifying... 表示前面技术进步带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: Research shows that when a bilingual person uses one language, the other is active at the same time.
sentences.translation: 研究表明，当双语者使用一种语言时，另一种语言也会同时处于活跃状态。

structure.text: Research
shows
that when a bilingual person uses one language, the other is active at the same time
when a bilingual person uses one language
the other is active at the same time

difficult_points: that 引导 shows 的宾语从句，承载研究结论。
when 从句嵌在 that 从句中，说明另一种语言活跃的条件。
the other 省略了 language，指双语者掌握的另一种语言。

sentences.sentence: When we hear a word, we don't hear the entire word all at once: the sounds arrive in sequential order.
sentences.translation: 当我们听到一个词时，并不是一下子听到整个词；声音是按顺序传来的。

structure.text: When we hear a word
we don't hear the entire word all at once
the sounds arrive in sequential order

difficult_points: 冒号后的分句解释前一句为什么说不是一次性听到整个词。
all at once 表示“一下子、同时”，与 sequential order 形成对照。
When 引导时间状语从句，主句是否定结构 don't hear。

sentences.sentence: Long before the word is finished, the brain's language system begins to guess what that word might be.
sentences.translation: 早在这个词还没有听完之前，大脑的语言系统就开始猜测这个词可能是什么。

structure.text: Long before the word is finished
the brain's language system
begins to guess
what that word might be

difficult_points: Long before 强调预测发生得很早，在单词完整结束前就已开始。
is finished 是被动语态，指这个词被听完或呈现完。
what 引导宾语从句，作 guess 的内容。

sentences.sentence: If you hear 'can', you will likely activate words like 'candy' and 'candle' as well, at least during the earlier stages of word recognition.
sentences.translation: 如果你听到“can”，你很可能也会激活像“candy”和“candle”这样的词，至少在词汇识别的早期阶段会如此。

structure.text: If you hear 'can'
you will likely activate words like 'candy' and 'candle' as well
at least during the earlier stages of word recognition

difficult_points: If 从句给出听觉输入，主句说明随之被激活的相关词。
will likely 表示较高可能性，不是绝对结果。
at least 限定该现象至少出现在词汇识别的早期阶段。

sentences.sentence: For bilingual people, this activation is not limited to a single language; auditory input activates corresponding words regardless of the language to which they belong.
sentences.translation: 对于双语者来说，这种激活并不限于单一语言；听觉输入会激活相应的词语，不管这些词属于哪一种语言。

structure.text: For bilingual people
this activation is not limited to a single language
auditory input activates corresponding words
regardless of the language to which they belong
to which they belong

difficult_points: 分号连接两个密切相关的独立分句，后一分句解释 activation 的跨语言范围。
be limited to 表示“局限于”，这里用否定说明不只激活一种语言。
to which they belong 是介词提前的定语从句，修饰 language。

sentences.sentence: Some of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon, called 'language co-activation', comes from studying eye movements.
sentences.translation: 关于这一现象的一些最有说服力的证据来自对眼动的研究，这一现象被称为“语言共同激活”。

structure.text: Some of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon
called 'language co-activation'
comes from studying eye movements
studying eye movements

difficult_points: called... 是过去分词短语，补充说明 this phenomenon 的名称。
主语中心是 Some，of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon 作后置限定。
from 后接动名词 studying，说明证据来源。

sentences.sentence: A Russian-English bilingual asked to 'pick up a marker' from a set of objects would look more at a stamp than someone who doesn't know Russian, because the Russian word for 'stamp', marka, sounds like the English word he or she heard, 'marker'.
sentences.translation: 如果让一名俄英双语者从一组物品中“拿起一支 marker”，他或她会比不懂俄语的人更多地看向一枚邮票，因为俄语中表示“邮票”的词 marka 听起来像他或她听到的英语词“marker”。

structure.text: A Russian-English bilingual
asked to 'pick up a marker' from a set of objects
would look more at a stamp than someone who doesn't know Russian
who doesn't know Russian
because the Russian word for 'stamp', marka, sounds like the English word he or she heard, 'marker'
he or she heard

difficult_points: asked to... 是过去分词短语，修饰 A Russian-English bilingual，相当于“被要求……的双语者”。
would look more... than... 表示实验中预期或典型的比较结果。
because 从句解释眼动差异的原因，其中 marka 是 the Russian word for 'stamp' 的同位说明。
he or she heard 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the English word。

sentences.sentence: In cases like this, language co-activation occurs because what the listener hears could map onto words in either language.
sentences.translation: 在这样的情况下，语言共同激活会发生，是因为听者所听到的内容可能对应到任一语言中的词。

structure.text: In cases like this
language co-activation occurs
because what the listener hears could map onto words in either language
what the listener hears
could map onto words in either language

difficult_points: because 引导原因状语从句，解释 co-activation 发生的机制。
what the listener hears 是名词性从句，在 because 从句中作主语。
either language 指双语者掌握的两种语言中的任何一种。

sentences.sentence: Having to deal with this persistent linguistic competition can result in difficulties, however.
sentences.translation: 然而，不得不应对这种持续的语言竞争也可能造成困难。

structure.text: Having to deal with this persistent linguistic competition
can result in difficulties
however

difficult_points: Having to deal with... 是动名词短语作主语，表示“必须处理……这件事”。
persistent linguistic competition 指两种语言长期同时活跃带来的竞争。
however 位于句末，标志本段开始转向讨论双语的成本。

sentences.sentence: For instance, knowing more than one language can cause speakers to name pictures more slowly, and can increase 'tip-of-the-tongue states', when you can almost, but not quite, bring a word to mind.
sentences.translation: 例如，掌握不止一种语言可能会使说话者给图片命名的速度变慢，也可能增加“话到嘴边却说不出”的状态，也就是你几乎能想起某个词却又差一点想不起来的时候。

structure.text: For instance
knowing more than one language
can cause speakers to name pictures more slowly
and can increase 'tip-of-the-tongue states'
when you can almost, but not quite, bring a word to mind

difficult_points: knowing more than one language 是动名词短语作主语，两个 can 并列说明其影响。
cause speakers to name... 使用 cause + 宾语 + 不定式结构。
when 引导的从句解释 'tip-of-the-tongue states' 的含义。
almost, but not quite 形成转折，表示“差一点但还没有”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 话到嘴边却说不出的状态

sentences.sentence: As a result, the constant juggling of two languages creates a need to control how much a person accesses a language at any given time.
sentences.translation: 因此，不断在两种语言之间切换会产生一种需求：控制一个人在任意特定时刻调用某种语言的程度。

structure.text: As a result
the constant juggling of two languages
creates a need to control
how much a person accesses a language at any given time

difficult_points: As a result 承接前文双语竞争带来的困难，表示结果。
the constant juggling of two languages 是比喻性表达，指持续协调两种语言。
how much 引导的名词性从句作 control 的宾语，表示控制调用语言的程度。

sentences.sentence: For this reason, bilingual people often perform better on tasks that require conflict management.
sentences.translation: 正因为如此，双语者在需要冲突管理的任务上往往表现更好。

structure.text: For this reason
bilingual people
often perform better on tasks
that require conflict management

difficult_points: For this reason 指代前文双语者需要控制语言调用这一原因。
perform better on tasks 中 on 表示在某类任务上的表现。
that require conflict management 是定语从句，限定 tasks 的类型。

sentences.sentence: In the classic Stroop Task, people see a word and are asked to name the colour of the word's font.
sentences.translation: 在经典的斯特鲁普任务中，人们看到一个词，并被要求说出这个词字体的颜色。

structure.text: In the classic Stroop Task
people
see a word
and are asked to name the colour of the word's font
to name the colour of the word's font

difficult_points: see 和 are asked 共享主语 people，构成并列动作。
are asked 是被动语态，说明实验任务由研究者提出。
the colour of the word's font 指字体颜色，而不是词本身表示的颜色。

sentences.sentence: When the colour and the word match (i.e., the word 'red' printed in red), people correctly name the colour more quickly than when the colour and the word don't match (i.e., the word 'red' printed in blue).
sentences.translation: 当颜色与词义一致时（即“red”这个词用红色印出），人们能比颜色与词义不一致时（即“red”这个词用蓝色印出）更快地正确说出颜色。

structure.text: When the colour and the word match
the word 'red' printed in red
people correctly name the colour more quickly
than when the colour and the word don't match
the word 'red' printed in blue

difficult_points: 两个 when 从句分别说明匹配和不匹配两种实验条件。
more quickly than... 构成比较结构，比较的是命名颜色的速度。
括号中的 i.e. 对前面的 match 和 don't match 进行具体举例说明。

expressions.text: the colour and the word match

sentences.sentence: This occurs because the word itself ('red') and its font colour (blue) conflict.
sentences.translation: 这是因为词本身（“red”）与它的字体颜色（蓝色）发生冲突。

structure.text: This occurs
because the word itself ('red') and its font colour (blue) conflict
the word itself ('red') and its font colour (blue)
conflict

difficult_points: This 回指上一句中不匹配条件下反应变慢的现象。
because 从句说明现象发生的原因。
compound subject 由 word itself 和 font colour 两部分组成，二者语义冲突。

sentences.sentence: Bilingual people often excel at tasks such as this, which tap into the ability to ignore competing perceptual information and focus on the relevant aspects of the input.
sentences.translation: 双语者往往擅长这类任务，因为这些任务会调用一种能力：忽略相互竞争的感知信息，并专注于输入中相关的方面。

structure.text: Bilingual people
often excel at tasks such as this
which tap into the ability to ignore competing perceptual information and focus on the relevant aspects of the input
to ignore competing perceptual information and focus on the relevant aspects of the input

difficult_points: excel at 表示“擅长”，后接 tasks 说明擅长的对象。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明这类任务测试了什么能力。
to ignore... and focus... 是并列不定式内容，共同说明 the ability。

sentences.sentence: Bilinguals are also better at switching between two tasks; for example, when bilinguals have to switch from categorizing objects by colour (red or green) to categorizing them by shape (circle or triangle), they do so more quickly than monolingual people, reflecting better cognitive control when having to make rapid changes of strategy.
sentences.translation: 双语者在两项任务之间切换时也表现更好；例如，当双语者必须从按颜色（红色或绿色）给物体分类，转为按形状（圆形或三角形）给物体分类时，他们比单语者做得更快，这反映出他们在必须迅速改变策略时具有更好的认知控制能力。

structure.text: Bilinguals are also better at switching between two tasks
when bilinguals have to switch from categorizing objects by colour (red or green) to categorizing them by shape (circle or triangle)
they do so more quickly than monolingual people
reflecting better cognitive control
when having to make rapid changes of strategy

difficult_points: 分号后 for example 引出具体实验任务，说明前面 switching between two tasks 的含义。
switch from... to... 构成固定搭配，表示从一种分类规则转到另一种。
do so 代替前面的 switch between two tasks，避免重复。
reflecting... 是现在分词短语，说明更快切换所反映出的能力。

sentences.sentence: It also seems that the neurological roots of the bilingual advantage extend to brain areas more traditionally associated with sensory processing.
sentences.translation: 双语优势的神经学根源似乎还延伸到那些传统上更多与感觉处理相关的大脑区域。

structure.text: It also seems
that the neurological roots of the bilingual advantage extend to brain areas more traditionally associated with sensory processing
the neurological roots of the bilingual advantage
extend to brain areas
more traditionally associated with sensory processing

difficult_points: It seems that... 中 It 是形式主语，that 从句表达真正内容。
also 表明本段是在前文认知控制优势之外增加神经层面的证据。
associated with... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 brain areas。

sentences.sentence: When monolingual and bilingual adolescents listen to simple speech sounds without any intervening background noise, they show highly similar brain stem responses.
sentences.translation: 当单语和双语青少年在没有任何干扰性背景噪音的情况下听简单语音时，他们表现出非常相似的脑干反应。

structure.text: When monolingual and bilingual adolescents listen to simple speech sounds without any intervening background noise
monolingual and bilingual adolescents
listen to simple speech sounds without any intervening background noise
they show highly similar brain stem responses

difficult_points: When 从句给出实验条件，主句说明在该条件下两组的脑反应。
without any intervening background noise 是介词短语，限定听音环境。
they 回指 monolingual and bilingual adolescents 两组青少年。

sentences.sentence: When researchers play the same sound to both groups in the presence of background noise, however, the bilingual listeners' neural response is considerably larger, reflecting better encoding of the sound's fundamental frequency, a feature of sound closely related to pitch perception.
sentences.translation: 然而，当研究人员在有背景噪音的情况下向两组播放同样的声音时，双语听者的神经反应明显更大，这反映出他们对声音基频的编码更好，而基频是与音高感知密切相关的声音特征。

structure.text: When researchers play the same sound to both groups in the presence of background noise
the bilingual listeners' neural response
is considerably larger
reflecting better encoding of the sound's fundamental frequency
a feature of sound closely related to pitch perception
closely related to pitch perception

difficult_points: When 从句设置有背景噪音的条件，however 显示该条件下两组差异出现。
considerably larger 表示差异程度很明显，不只是稍大。
reflecting... 是现在分词短语，说明神经反应更大的含义。
a feature of sound... 是同位语，解释 fundamental frequency。

sentences.sentence: Such improvements in cognitive and sensory processing may help a bilingual person to process information in the environment, and help explain why bilingual adults acquire a third language better than monolingual adults master a second language.
sentences.translation: 认知和感觉处理方面的这些提升，可能有助于双语者处理环境中的信息，并有助于解释为什么双语成年人学习第三语言比单语成年人掌握第二语言更容易。

structure.text: Such improvements in cognitive and sensory processing
may help a bilingual person to process information in the environment
and help explain
why bilingual adults acquire a third language better than monolingual adults master a second language
better than monolingual adults master a second language

difficult_points: Such improvements 回指前文双语者在认知和感觉处理上的提升。
两个 help 共用主语 Such improvements，分别接不定式和省略 to 的 explain。
why 引导名词性从句，作 explain 的宾语。
better than... 比较的是双语成人学习第三语言与单语成人掌握第二语言的表现。

sentences.sentence: This advantage may be rooted in the skill of focusing on information about the new language while reducing interference from the languages they already know.
sentences.translation: 这种优势可能植根于一种能力：专注于有关新语言的信息，同时减少他们已掌握语言所带来的干扰。

structure.text: This advantage
may be rooted in the skill
of focusing on information about the new language
while reducing interference from the languages they already know
they already know

difficult_points: This advantage 指上一句提到的双语成人学习第三语言的优势。
be rooted in 表示“根植于”，用于解释优势来源。
while reducing... 表示与 focusing 同时发生的动作。
they already know 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 languages。

sentences.sentence: Research also indicates that bilingual experience may help to keep the cognitive mechanisms sharp by recruiting alternate brain networks to compensate for those that become damaged during aging.
sentences.translation: 研究还表明，双语经历可能通过调用替代性大脑网络来补偿衰老过程中受损的那些网络，从而帮助保持认知机制的敏锐。

structure.text: Research
also indicates
that bilingual experience may help to keep the cognitive mechanisms sharp
by recruiting alternate brain networks
to compensate for those that become damaged during aging
that become damaged during aging

difficult_points: that 引导 indicates 的宾语从句，说明研究发现。
keep the cognitive mechanisms sharp 使用 keep + 宾语 + 形容词补足语。
by recruiting... 表示保持认知敏锐的方式。
those 指代 brain networks，后面的 that 从句说明哪些网络需要被补偿。

sentences.sentence: Older bilinguals enjoy improved memory relative to monolingual people, which can lead to real-world health benefits.
sentences.translation: 与单语者相比，年长的双语者拥有更好的记忆力，这可能带来现实中的健康益处。

structure.text: Older bilinguals
enjoy improved memory relative to monolingual people
relative to monolingual people
which can lead to real-world health benefits

difficult_points: enjoy 在此不是“享受”，而是表示“拥有某种有利条件”。
relative to... 引出比较基准，即单语者。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代前面“记忆力更好”这一整件事。

sentences.sentence: In a study of over 200 patients with Alzheimer's disease, a degenerative brain disease, bilingual patients reported showing initial symptoms of the disease an average of five years later than monolingual patients.
sentences.translation: 在一项针对 200 多名阿尔茨海默病患者的研究中，双语患者报告称，他们出现该病初始症状的时间平均比单语患者晚五年；阿尔茨海默病是一种退行性脑部疾病。

structure.text: In a study of over 200 patients with Alzheimer's disease
a degenerative brain disease
bilingual patients
reported showing initial symptoms of the disease an average of five years later than monolingual patients
showing initial symptoms of the disease
an average of five years later than monolingual patients

difficult_points: a degenerative brain disease 是 Alzheimer's disease 的同位语，解释疾病性质。
reported showing... 中 showing 是动名词，表示报告的具体内容。
an average of five years later than... 是比较结构，说明症状出现时间的平均差距。

expressions.meaning_zh: 一项针对 200 多名患者的研究

sentences.sentence: In a follow-up study, researchers compared the brains of bilingual and monolingual patients matched on the severity of Alzheimer's symptoms.
sentences.translation: 在一项后续研究中，研究人员比较了双语和单语患者的大脑，这些患者在阿尔茨海默病症状的严重程度上相匹配。

structure.text: In a follow-up study
researchers
compared the brains of bilingual and monolingual patients
matched on the severity of Alzheimer's symptoms

difficult_points: follow-up study 指在前一研究基础上继续进行的后续研究。
matched on... 是过去分词短语，修饰 patients，说明两组患者在症状严重程度上可比。
the brains of bilingual and monolingual patients 中 of 短语限定比较对象。

sentences.sentence: Surprisingly, the bilinguals' brains had more physical signs of disease than their monolingual counterparts, even though their outward behaviour and abilities were the same.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，与单语者对应群体相比，双语者的大脑有更多疾病的生理迹象，尽管他们的外在行为和能力是一样的。

structure.text: Surprisingly
the bilinguals' brains
had more physical signs of disease than their monolingual counterparts
even though their outward behaviour and abilities were the same
their outward behaviour and abilities

difficult_points: Surprisingly 表明这一结果与预期相反：脑部病理更多但表现没有更差。
more... than... 比较双语者与单语对应患者的大脑病理迹象。
even though 引导让步从句，强调外在行为能力相同与脑部病理更多之间的反差。

sentences.sentence: If the brain is an engine, bilingualism may help it to go farther on the same amount of fuel.
sentences.translation: 如果把大脑比作一台发动机，那么双语能力可能帮助它用同样多的燃料走得更远。

structure.text: If the brain is an engine
bilingualism
may help it to go farther
on the same amount of fuel

difficult_points: If 从句引入比喻框架，把大脑比作发动机。
it 回指 the brain，而不是 bilingualism。
on the same amount of fuel 延续发动机比喻，表示在同等资源下维持更久功能。

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, the benefits associated with bilingual experience seem to start very early.
sentences.translation: 此外，与双语经历相关的好处似乎很早就开始显现。

structure.text: Furthermore
the benefits associated with bilingual experience
associated with bilingual experience
seem to start very early

difficult_points: Furthermore 递进引出双语好处在年龄上的新证据。
associated with... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 benefits。
seem to start very early 表示研究推断，不是绝对断言。

sentences.sentence: In one study, researchers taught seven-month-old babies growing up in monolingual or bilingual homes that when they heard a tinkling sound, a puppet appeared on one side of a screen.
sentences.translation: 在一项研究中，研究人员教导在单语或双语家庭中成长的七个月大婴儿：当他们听到叮当声时，一个木偶会出现在屏幕的一侧。

structure.text: In one study
researchers
taught seven-month-old babies growing up in monolingual or bilingual homes
growing up in monolingual or bilingual homes
that when they heard a tinkling sound, a puppet appeared on one side of a screen
when they heard a tinkling sound

difficult_points: growing up... 是现在分词短语，修饰 babies，说明婴儿的语言家庭环境。
taught 后接 babies 和 that 从句，表示让婴儿学习一个声音与视觉事件的规则。
when 从句嵌在 that 从句中，说明木偶出现的触发条件。

sentences.sentence: Halfway through the study, the puppet began appearing on the opposite side of the screen.
sentences.translation: 研究进行到一半时，木偶开始出现在屏幕的另一侧。

structure.text: Halfway through the study
the puppet
began appearing on the opposite side of the screen
on the opposite side of the screen

difficult_points: Halfway through... 是时间状语，表示实验规则在中途发生改变。
began appearing 后接动名词，强调木偶开始反复出现在新位置。
opposite side 与上一句 one side 形成对照。

sentences.sentence: In order to get a reward, the infants had to adjust the rule they'd learned; only the bilingual babies were able to successfully learn the new rule.
sentences.translation: 为了获得奖励，婴儿必须调整他们已经学会的规则；只有双语婴儿能够成功学会新规则。

structure.text: In order to get a reward
the infants had to adjust the rule they'd learned
they'd learned
only the bilingual babies were able to successfully learn the new rule
to successfully learn the new rule

difficult_points: In order to... 表示目的，说明婴儿调整规则的动机。
had to 表示实验任务中的必要条件。
they'd learned 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the rule。
only 放在主语前，强调成功适应新规则的只是一组婴儿。

sentences.sentence: This suggests that for very young children, as well as for older people, navigating a multilingual environment imparts advantages that transfer far beyond language.
sentences.translation: 这表明，对于很小的儿童以及年长者而言，在多语环境中生活和应对会带来远远超出语言本身的优势。

structure.text: This suggests
that for very young children, as well as for older people, navigating a multilingual environment imparts advantages that transfer far beyond language
for very young children, as well as for older people
navigating a multilingual environment
imparts advantages
that transfer far beyond language

difficult_points: This 回指前一句双语婴儿更能学习新规则的实验结果。
that 从句是 suggests 的宾语从句，给出研究者从实验中得到的推论。
navigating a multilingual environment 是动名词短语作 that 从句中的主语。
as well as for older people 将年幼儿童与前文提到的年长者联系起来。

expressions.meaning_zh: 迁移到远超语言本身的领域
